![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

`RemoteRegistry.load` で取得した外部 JSON データに対してスキーマ検証が行われていません。不正な形式のマニフェストを読み込んだ場合、`resolve` 実行時に予期せぬランタイムエラーが発生する可能性があります。プロジェクトで導入済みの `zod` 等を使用して、ロード時に構造をチェックすることを推奨します。

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

`stockfish` 以外のエンジン（`yaneuraou`, `katago` 等）の SRI ハッシュがプレースホルダー (`sha384-SetCorrect...`) のままになっています。セキュリティ整合性チェックを有効にした場合にロードに失敗するため、正しいハッシュ値を設定するか、未対応のリソースについては SRI を無効化する設定（`__unsafeNoSRI`）を検討してください。

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**`message` フィールドに英語の文字列がハードコードされています。**

コーディングガイドラインの i18n 規則「ユーザー向け文字列のハードコードを禁止し、`locales/` 配下の多言語対応ファイルの使用を求めること」に抵触します。`i18nKey` は設定されていますが、`message` フィールドも `locales/` の翻訳文字列から動的に取得するか、開発用フォールバックであることを明示すべきです。



As per coding guidelines: `**/*.{ts,tsx}`: ユーザー向け文字列のハードコードを禁止し、`locales/` 配下の多言語対応ファイルの使用を求めること。

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@packages/adapter-ensemble/src/strategies/BestScoreStrategy.ts` around lines
22 - 28, The EngineError creation in BestScoreStrategy (the block that checks
results.length === 0) uses a hardcoded English message; replace the hardcoded
message with a localized string lookup from the project's locales (or explicitly
mark it as a dev-only fallback) and pass that into the EngineError message field
while keeping the existing i18nKey (EngineErrorCode.VALIDATION_ERROR, i18nKey:
"adapters.ensemble.errors.noResults"); update the code to call the appropriate
i18n accessor used across the codebase (e.g., i18n.t / translate /
getLocaleString) to populate message so no user-facing English is hardcoded.
```

</details>

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**コンストラクターで受け取った `weights` が `aggregateResults` 内で一切使用されていません。**

`this.weights` は格納されますが（Line 21）、`aggregateResults` 内では `const weight = 1.0` としてハードコードされており、`this.weights` への参照がありません。これにより `new WeightedStrategy({ "stockfish": 2.0 })` のように重みを設定したユーザーが意図した動作を得られず、常に均一な重みで動作します。

インターフェース改修（TODO）が完了するまでは、コンストラクターの `weights` パラメーターを受け付けないか、非空オブジェクトが渡された場合に警告を発することを推奨します。

<details>
<summary>🛡️ 暫定対応の提案</summary>

```diff
  constructor(weights: Record<string, number> = {}) {
    this.weights = weights;
+   if (Object.keys(weights).length > 0) {
+     console.warn(
+       "[WeightedStrategy] Engine-specific weights are not yet applied. " +
+       "All engines are weighted uniformly until the interface is updated (see TODO)."
+     );
+   }
  }
```
</details>

```
- 22, The constructor stores weights but aggregateResults ignores them (it
hardcodes const weight = 1.0); update aggregateResults in class WeightedStrategy
to look up the per-model weight from this.weights (e.g., const weight =
this.weights[modelKey] ?? 1.0) so provided weights like new WeightedStrategy({
"stockfish": 2.0 }) are applied, and add a guard/log in the constructor to warn
if a non-empty weights object is passed but contains unknown model keys to help
catch config mistakes.
```

✅ Addressed in commit 909faa1

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**非Stockfishエンジンに無効なプレースホルダーSRIハッシュが使用されています**

`yaneuraou`、`katago`、`edax`、`mortal` の全アセットで SRI ハッシュが `sha384-SetCorrectHashHereToSatisfySecurityAudit0123456789ABCDEF01234567` という同一プレースホルダー値のままになっています。文字列長は SHA-384 の仕様を満たしていますが、実際のファイルコンテンツから計算されたハッシュではありません。

このままでは以下の二択になります：
- SRI検証が実施される → 当該エンジンは全てロード失敗（機能停止）
- SRI検証がスキップされる → セキュリティポリシー違反

PR目標「Enforce SRI hash verification for all resource loading operations」と直接矛盾します。各 URL に対応する実際の SHA-384 ハッシュを計算して設定してください。





Also applies to: 39-39, 55-55, 60-60, 76-76, 92-92

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@packages/registry/data/engines.json` at line 34, The SRI fields for
non-Stockfish engines (yaneuraou, katago, edax, mortal) currently use a
placeholder value ("sha384-SetCorrectHashHere...") and must be replaced with
real SHA-384 SRI values; for each asset entry in
packages/registry/data/engines.json find the "sri" property for the listed
engines and compute the SHA-384 hash of the actual file served at the asset URL,
encode the digest in base64 and set the value to "sha384-<base64digest>"
(preserve the "sha384-" prefix and string format), and update every occurrence
noted (the other entries referenced) so SRI validation will succeed.
```

</details>

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**コンストラクタの `data as Record<string, unknown>` キャストに実行時検証がありません**

`data: unknown` を受け取りながら `as Record<string, unknown>` で直接キャストしており、無効な入力（例：`null`、プリミティブ型）が渡された場合でも型エラーを発生させません。`StaticRegistry` 自体はデフォルト値がバンドル済み JSON であるため低リスクですが、`RemoteRegistry` のような継承クラスへ `super({})` を通じて伝播するため、最低限 `object` であることの guard を追加してください。

<details>
<summary>🛡️ 修正案</summary>

```diff
   constructor(data: unknown = enginesData) {
-    this.data = data as Record<string, unknown>;
+    if (data === null || typeof data !== "object" || Array.isArray(data)) {
+      throw new TypeError("[StaticRegistry] data must be a plain object");
+    }
+    this.data = data as Record<string, unknown>;
   }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@packages/registry/src/index.ts` around lines 10 - 12, The constructor
currently casts data: unknown directly to Record<string, unknown> which allows
null/primitives to slip through; update the constructor in the class
(referencing constructor, this.data, enginesData, StaticRegistry and
RemoteRegistry callers) to validate at runtime that data is a non-null object
(e.g. typeof data === "object" && data !== null) before assigning; if validation
fails, fall back to the bundled enginesData (or throw a clear error) so
inherited classes like RemoteRegistry receiving super({}) cannot propagate
invalid values.
```

</details>

**`result.score` の型アサーションを型ガードに置き換えてください。**

`IBaseSearchResult` のインデックスシグネチャ `[key: string]: unknown` により、`score` プロパティは `unknown` 型です。`as IScoreInfo` で直接キャストすると、実行時に不正な形状のデータが入り得るため、型ガードで検証してから使用してください。

<details>
<summary>修正例</summary>

```diff
+const isScoreInfo = (value: unknown): value is IScoreInfo => {
+  if (typeof value !== "object" || value === null) return false;
+  const v = value as { cp?: unknown; mate?: unknown; points?: unknown; winrate?: unknown };
+  return (
+    typeof v.cp === "number" ||
+    typeof v.mate === "number" ||
+    typeof v.points === "number" ||
+    typeof v.winrate === "number"
+  );
+};
```

```diff
-      const score = result.score as IScoreInfo | undefined;
+      const score = isScoreInfo(result.score) ? result.score : undefined;
```

```
with a runtime type guard: implement a predicate function (e.g., isScoreInfo)
that checks result.score is an object and has the expected shape/fields of
IScoreInfo, use that guard before accessing score fields, and skip/continue when
the guard returns false; remove the "as IScoreInfo" assertion and rely on the
guard to narrow the type for safe usage in the rest of the loop.
```

+---

+
+# CodeRabbit Code Review
+
+AI-powered code review using CodeRabbit. Enables developers to implement features, review code, and fix issues in autonomous cycles without manual intervention.
+
+## Capabilities
+
+- Finds bugs, security issues, and quality risks in changed code
+- Groups findings by severity (Critical, Warning, Info)
+- Works on staged, committed, or all changes; supports base branch/commit
+- Provides fix suggestions (`--plain`) or minimal output for agents (`--prompt-only`)
+
+## When to Use
+
+When user asks to:
+
+- Review code changes / Review my code
+- Check code quality / Find bugs or security issues
+- Get PR feedback / Pull request review
+- What's wrong with my code / my changes
+- Run coderabbit / Use coderabbit
+
+## How to Review
+
+### 1. Check Prerequisites
+
+```bash
+coderabbit --version 2>/dev/null || echo "NOT_INSTALLED"
+coderabbit auth status 2>&1
+```
+
+**If CLI not installed**, tell user:
+
+```text
+Please install CodeRabbit CLI first:
+curl -fsSL https://cli.coderabbit.ai/install.sh | sh
+```
+
+**If not authenticated**, tell user:
+
+```text
+Please authenticate first:
+coderabbit auth login
+```
+
+### 2. Run Review
+
+Use `--prompt-only` for minimal output optimized for AI agents:
+
+```bash
+coderabbit review --prompt-only
+```
+
+Or use `--plain` for detailed feedback with fix suggestions:
+
+```bash
+coderabbit review --plain
+```
+
+**Options:**
+
+| Flag             | Description                            |
+| ---------------- | -------------------------------------- |
+| `-t all`         | All changes (default)                  |
+| `-t committed`   | Committed changes only                 |
+| `-t uncommitted` | Uncommitted changes only               |
+| `--base main`    | Compare against specific branch        |
+| `--base-commit`  | Compare against specific commit hash   |
+| `--prompt-only`  | Minimal output optimized for AI agents |
+| `--plain`        | Detailed feedback with fix suggestions |
+
+**Shorthand:** `cr` is an alias for `coderabbit`:
+
+```bash
+cr review --prompt-only
+```
+
+### 3. Present Results
+
+Group findings by severity:
+
+1. **Critical** - Security vulnerabilities, data loss risks, crashes
+2. **Warning** - Bugs, performance issues, anti-patterns
+3. **Info** - Style issues, suggestions, minor improvements
+
+Create a task list for issues found that need to be addressed.
+
+### 4. Fix Issues (Autonomous Workflow)
+
+When user requests implementation + review:
+
+1. Implement the requested feature
+2. Run `coderabbit review --prompt-only`
+3. Create task list from findings
+4. Fix critical and warning issues systematically
+5. Re-run review to verify fixes
+6. Repeat until clean or only info-level issues remain
+

🌐 Web query:

`React inline style property naming rules camelCase CSS properties`

💡 Result:

In React, the `style` prop takes a JavaScript object whose keys follow DOM/CSSOM naming (not raw CSS text) [1]:

- **Use `camelCase` for multi-word CSS properties:** `background-color` → `backgroundColor`, `font-weight` → `fontWeight` [1][2].  
- **Single-word properties stay lowercase:** `color`, `margin`, etc. [1]  
- **Special case:** CSS `float` becomes **`cssFloat`** (since `float` is a JS keyword) [2].  
- **Vendor prefixes are also camelCased and correctly capitalized:** e.g. `WebkitTransition` (capital “W”), and `msTransition` (lowercase “m”) [3].

**Examples**
```jsx
<div style={{ backgroundColor: "red", fontWeight: 600, cssFloat: "left" }} />
<div style={{ WebkitTransition: "all 200ms", msTransition: "all 200ms" }} />
```

Sources: [1] React docs (Common components) ([react.dev](https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/components/common?utm_source=openai)), [2] MDN (CSSStyleProperties / CSSOM naming) ([developer.mozilla.org](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CSSStyleProperties?utm_source=openai)), [3] vendor prefix capitalization rule ([docs.rencore.com](https://docs.rencore.com/spcaf/v7/RCT010110_ReactDOMElementsStylePropertyRule.html?utm_source=openai))

🌐 Web query:

`Are spaces allowed in CSS property names React style objects`

💡 Result:

No.

In React’s `style={{ ... }}` object, keys must be valid CSS property identifiers (written in `camelCase`, e.g. `backgroundColor`). Property names with spaces aren’t valid CSS, so they won’t work. ([react.dev](https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/components/common?utm_source=openai))

If you need a non-camelCase CSS name, you can use a *string key* (e.g. `style={{ "margin-top": 8 }}`), but it still can’t contain spaces. ([developer.mozilla.org](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CSSStyleProperties?utm_source=openai))

CSS custom properties are supported via string keys like `style={{ "--my-property": "10px" }}`—again, no spaces in the name. ([developer.mozilla.org](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Guides/Cascading_variables/Using_custom_properties?utm_source=openai))
